title: The Control Room
description: Navigate complex logic gates using Loops, Arrays, and Control Flow.

questions.question: Which of these perfectly translates 'If health is greater than 0 AND armor is not 0'?
When building an if-statement, any additional 'fallback' condition checks use the `else _____` keywords.
questions.explanation: else if (condition) checks a secondary branch.

questions.question: If you don't include a `break;` at the end of a `switch` case, what happens?
options: It 'falls through' and continues running the code inside the NEXT case below it.
questions.explanation: The break is essential to tell the computer to jump out of the switch block so it doesn't accidentally execute the code of following cases.

questions.question: A while loop checking `while (true)` will loop forever.
questions.explanation: Yes. This explicitly creates an infinite loop since 'true' holds true permanently.

questions.question: What mathematical construct is a `for` loop perfectly designed to iterate through?
questions.explanation: Because arrays are zero-indexed `[0],[1],[2]`, a for-loop effortlessly counts indices from `i=0` to `i<Max`.

questions.question: A group of loops placed inside of other loops are called _____ loops.
questions.explanation: Often used for navigating two-dimensional grids like maps or tables.

questions.question: What is the physical size limit of elements an array can hold?
options: It depends on the developer's initialization declaration, ex: int nums[50];
questions.explanation: You statically declare the size upon array creation, fixing its memory boundary forever.

questions.question: String variables are uniquely treated as objects with hidden properties. You can natively call functions like `password.length()` directly off them.
questions.explanation: Yes, strings have built-in methods provided by standard C++ libraries.

questions.question: If a string is just an array, how would you change `word = "Cat"` into `Bat`?
questions.explanation: Grabbing index 0 grabs the 'C', overwriting it with 'B' turns the base string into "Bat".

questions.question: Inside a `switch` block, the final fallback code that runs if no cases successfully match is designated by the `_____:` keyword.
questions.explanation: Default runs when nothing else did.
